title: rubus × inops
intro: Rubus × inops, also known as the black raspberry or black bramble, is a hybrid plant resulting from the cross between Rubus occidentalis (black raspberry) and Rubus idaeus (red raspberry). The Rubus × inops plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of antioxidants, anthocyanins, and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus × inops include its potential to prevent cardiovascular disease, certain cancers, and age-related cognitive decline, as well as its ability to support digestive health and immune function. Rubus × inops is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, juices, and capsules, which can be consumed fresh or dried to leverage its medicinal and health-promoting properties.
